title: using the podman Python API freezes it all
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I need to use the Python API to monitor the running containers; what I observe at this point is:

#### before
* from terminal 1, running `podman ps` works instantaneously; `pgrep podman` returns no entry

#### trigger the issue
* in terminal 2, I start a Python interpreter and simply run 

```
with podman.Client() as client:
   ...:     list(client.containers.list())
```

**Describe the results you received:**

#### after
* the Python command does not return and hangs forever
* similarly a `podman ps` command at that point in terminal 1 hangs as well
* `pgrep podman` exhibits one process
```
ps $(pgrep podman)
    PID TTY      STAT   TIME COMMAND
 328282 ?        Ssl   32:03 /usr/bin/podman varlink unix:/run/podman/io.podman --timeout=60000
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect the Python statement to return very quickly with a list of my running containers, and `podman ps` to be able to list running containers at all times

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

As far as I can tell this happens every time.

I am experiencing this on the same vanilla f31 box as the one that exhibited #4600, and at first I thought this could even be related; however a pretty version of `/proc/328282/environ` shows this

```
LANG=en_US.UTF-8
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin
LISTEN_PID=328282
LISTEN_FDS=1
LISTEN_FDNAMES=io.podman.socket
INVOCATION_ID=230f954c0ac44788ba4e3917d55dae01
JOURNAL_STREAM=9:2071639
```

Maybe relevant also, the way I have configured podman wrt systemd is to have both units `io.podman.service` and `io.podman.socket` enabled

Finally, an `strace` session on that 328282 varlink thingy reports an endless stream of 

```
<snip> 
futex(0xc0004ca4c8, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x562dbb0259e8, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL) = 0
futex(0x562dbb0259e8, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL) = 0
<snip>
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.1
  podman version: 1.6.2
host:

      crun version 0.10.6
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

  hostname: thermals

  rootless: false

    number: 104
  GraphDriverName: overlay

    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  GraphRoot: /nbhosting/containers
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: btrfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
# rpm -aq | grep podman
podman-docker-1.6.2-2.fc31.noarch
podman-manpages-1.6.2-2.fc31.noarch
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
# pip freeze | grep podman
podman==1.6.0
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

physical box running vanilla fedora31; upgraded from at least fedora27, used to run docker instead of podman

it feels like I have something grossly misconfigured..

comments.body:
I have just tried to replace the pip-installed podman module with the one I just noticed in dnf

```
 1027  pip uninstall podman
 1028  dnf install python3-podman-api
```

but observe the very same behaviour
@jwhonce if you'd like any additional information, please feel free to ask - thanks !

I would appreciate any kind of feedback on this issue, that is a hard stopper for me

I would happily need to know at least 
* whether this is a bug indeed, 
* or whether I am misinterpreting something and am having wrong expectations about how the system behaves

thanks !
Are you running the python as root?  The service is listening as root, and should only be communicated with via root.
Yes everything is run locally from within the f31 box, and everything runs as root at this point
if that's of any interest I am presently on irc and available for any fumbling about that could help shedding any light on this

I've been trying to understand this issue a little better on my side

((for the record I have taken the time to completely wipe out the podman install, i.e. rpm uninstalled, cleaned the containers area completely, in short scrubbed as much as I could before reinstalling))

I need to amend the issue topic; turns out the Python API call **does return eventually** after a **very long while**; here's the code that I run on the Python side

```
In [6]: def bench():
   ...:     now = time.time()
   ...:     with podman.Client() as client:
   ...:         containers = list(client.containers.list())
   ...:     print(containers)
   ...:     duration = time.time()-now
   ...:     print(f"took {duration:2g}s")
```

and, the `containers` variable comes up with **valid information** (at that time I have a single container running) :

```
In [7]: bench()
[{'id': 'dda5ec297950654b2088e7ef884a5305f5f656bbecdeba909ca4cdb02c67594b', 'image': 'localhost/python3-s2:latest', 'imageid': '9479daf896a7bc30e390bf4dde68f5e763bda0fa7d263a183617029255a3a436', 'command': ['tini', '-g', '--', 'start-in-dir-as-uid.sh', '/home/jovyan', '100277', 'jupyter', 'notebook', '--ip=0.0.0.0', '--no-browser', '--NotebookApp.notebook_dir=/home/jovyan/work', '--NotebookApp.token=python3-s2-x-57f9dd0f5709a11d694073f0c3d11fdf', '--NotebookApp.base_url=/43493/', '--log-level=DEBUG'], 'createdat': '2020-01-07T19:19:09Z', 'runningfor': '14h35m13.968377532s', 'status': 'running', 'ports': [{'host_port': '43493', 'host_ip': '', 'protocol': 'tcp', 'container_port': '8888'}], 'rootfssize': 4206911091, 'rwsize': 308451205, 'names': 'python3-s2-x-57f9dd0f5709a11d694073f0c3d11fdf', 'labels': {'maintainer': 'Jupyter Project <jupyter@googlegroups.com>'}, 'mounts': [{'destination': '/home/jovyan/.jupyter/jupyter_notebook_config.py', 'type': 'bind', 'source': '/nbhosting/dev/jupyter/python3-s2/jupyter_notebook_config.py', 'options': ['ro', 'rprivate', 'rbind']}, {'destination': '/home/jovyan/.jupyter/custom', 'type': 'bind', 'source': '/nbhosting/dev/jupyter/python3-s2', 'options': ['ro', 'rprivate', 'rbind']}, {'destination': '/home/jovyan/modules', 'type': 'bind', 'source': '/nbhosting/dev/modules/python3-s2', 'options': ['ro', 'rprivate', 'rbind']}, {'destination': '/home/jovyan/static', 'type': 'bind', 'source': '/nbhosting/dev/static/python3-s2', 'options': ['ro', 'rprivate', 'rbind']}, {'destination': '/nbhosting/dev/courses-git/python3-s2', 'type': 'bind', 'source': '/nbhosting/dev/courses-git/python3-s2', 'options': ['ro', 'rprivate', 'rbind']}, {'destination': '/home/jovyan/work', 'type': 'bind', 'source': '/nbhosting/dev/students/57f9dd0f5709a11d694073f0c3d11fdf/python3-s2', 'options': ['rw', 'rprivate', 'rbind']}, {'destination': '/proc', 'type': 'proc', 'source': 'proc', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev']}, {'destination': '/dev', 'type': 'tmpfs', 'source': 'tmpfs', 'options': ['nosuid', 'strictatime', 'mode=755', 'size=65536k']}, {'destination': '/dev/pts', 'type': 'devpts', 'source': 'devpts', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'newinstance', 'ptmxmode=0666', 'mode=0620', 'gid=5']}, {'destination': '/dev/mqueue', 'type': 'mqueue', 'source': 'mqueue', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev']}, {'destination': '/sys', 'type': 'sysfs', 'source': 'sysfs', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev', 'ro']}, {'destination': '/sys/fs/cgroup', 'type': 'cgroup', 'source': 'cgroup', 'options': ['rprivate', 'nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev', 'relatime', 'ro']}], 'containerrunning': True, 'namespaces': {'user': '4026531837', 'uts': '4026533015', 'pidns': '4026533017', 'pid': '87567', 'cgroup': '4026531835', 'net': '4026532759', 'mnt': '4026533014', 'ipc': '4026533016'}, 'running': True}]
took 113.915s
```

several measurements exhibit a duration for the API call that all are in the 110-120s range:
```
took 118.627s
took 113.915s
took 113.431s
took 115.71s
```

I'm having a hard time figuring what can possibly cause the API to hold the data for so long, especially considering that it eventually serves it allright ...

***

I was also, probably unrelated but still, a bit curious about this `timeout` setting below
In which circumstances exactly is that timeout likely to trigger ?
should I try messing with that ?


```
# systemctl cat io.podman.service

# /usr/lib/systemd/system/io.podman.service
[Unit]
<snip>

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman varlink unix:%t/podman/io.podman --timeout=60000
TimeoutStopSec=30
KillMode=process

[Install]
<snip>
```

now, if instead of running one of my containers, I spawn one in a simpler way

```
podman run -ti --rm ubuntu
```

then in this case the API behaves more or less as expected

```
In [6]: bench()
[{'id': '91530acb3301b6d8ed03f57c8952d6500200fd76638b4236fa380ee095439405', 'image': 'docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest', 'imageid': '549b9b86cb8d75a2b668c21c50ee092716d070f129fd1493f95ab7e43767eab8', 'command': ['/bin/bash'], 'createdat': '2020-01-08T14:21:38+01:00', 'runningfor': '7.801287879s', 'status': 'running', 'ports': [], 'rootfssize': 64194718, 'rwsize': 0, 'names': 'practical_montalcini', 'labels': {}, 'mounts': [{'destination': '/proc', 'type': 'proc', 'source': 'proc', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev']}, {'destination': '/dev', 'type': 'tmpfs', 'source': 'tmpfs', 'options': ['nosuid', 'strictatime', 'mode=755', 'size=65536k']}, {'destination': '/dev/pts', 'type': 'devpts', 'source': 'devpts', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'newinstance', 'ptmxmode=0666', 'mode=0620', 'gid=5']}, {'destination': '/dev/mqueue', 'type': 'mqueue', 'source': 'mqueue', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev']}, {'destination': '/sys', 'type': 'sysfs', 'source': 'sysfs', 'options': ['nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev', 'ro']}, {'destination': '/sys/fs/cgroup', 'type': 'cgroup', 'source': 'cgroup', 'options': ['rprivate', 'nosuid', 'noexec', 'nodev', 'relatime', 'ro']}], 'containerrunning': True, 'namespaces': {'user': '4026531837', 'uts': '4026533015', 'pidns': '4026533017', 'pid': '353875', 'cgroup': '4026531835', 'net': '4026532759', 'mnt': '4026533014', 'ipc': '4026533016'}, 'running': True}]
took 0.943457s
```

although a duration of nearly 1s to probe for one single container does sound odd, it's clearly more workable than almost minutes :)

so this points at the way the container is started

I could try and isolate what differences, in the ways both containers are started, that triggers the problem; any recommendation as to what could be the most likely culprit here ?

as per @jwhonce instructions on irc, I have tried to run `podman inspect -s` on both containers

the outcome is that on the regular ubuntu container `inspect -s` returns relatively fast, while on my custom containers it hangs for the approximate same duration as the API call

when *strace*'ing that `podman inspect` process, I can see a great many deal of calls to `futex()`like typically 

```
<snip>
sched_yield()                           = 0
futex(0x5634b261bc30, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 0
futex(0x5634b261bc30, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x5634b261bd48, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, {tv_sec=0, tv_nsec=100000}) = -1 EAGAIN (Resource temporarily unavailable)
epoll_pwait(4, [], 128, 0, NULL, 140736723234360) = 0
futex(0xc000ab4148, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x5634b2646520, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x5634b261bc30, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1
futex(0x5634b26289e8, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL) = 0
futex(0x5634b26289e8, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL) = 0
futex(0xc0004fa148, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1epoll_pwait(4, [], 128, 0, NULL, 140736723234360) = 0
<snip>
```

@mheon 
`podman mount` on the custom container returns quickly within 1/10 s

as a conclusion on this issue, it seems that the time taken by the API call is mostly spent in figuring the hard-disk space used by the container

an evidence of that is the presence of `'rootfssize': 64194718` in the data structure returned by the API

Transfering to podman-python to see if there is / suggest a way for the API call to specify that this information is not needed

https://github.com/containers/python-podman/issues/72
